disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical fixed target lesions with three concentric zones on the hands/forearms spreading to the trunk
trigger_question_examples: Do the spots have three distinct rings (a bullseye), and are they in the same place (not moving/fading in hours)?
Are they symmetrical on both hands/arms?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, they're fixed bullseye spots with three rings, symmetrical on both hands and arms.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sores/blisters in the mouth, eyes or genitals; no high fever; no skin peeling in sheets (SJS/TEN screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any painful sores or blisters in your mouth, eyes or genitals, a high fever, or skin peeling off in sheets?
Are your lips/eyes affected or is the skin sloughing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No mouth, eye or genital sores, no high fever, and no skin peeling — just the bullseye spots.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A cold sore on the lip about 10 days before the rash (preceding trigger)
trigger_question_examples: Did you have a cold sore or any infection in the week or two before this started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I had a cold sore on my lip about 10 days ago; no new medicines.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a dangerous allergic reaction? It looks terrifying and it's spreading fast.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure appropriately AFTER a careful mucosal/systemic screen: if no mucosal involvement/fever/sloughing, this is a benign self-limiting HSV-triggered reaction. Must still give an explicit SJS/TEN safety-net rather than blanket reassurance without screening.
